title: Men's March Madness 2026: Ranking the Final Four teams
content: Four teams will play for it all next weekend. Let's rank Michigan, Arizona, Illinois and UConn one final time.

title: Mullins' last-second 3 sends UConn to Final Four
content: UConn eliminated No. 1 overall seed Duke from the men's NCAA tournament on Braylon Mullins' 3-pointer with 0.4 seconds left.

title: Eagles GM offers same reply to every Brown query
content: Eagles general manager Howie Roseman had a stock answer for every question about receiver A.J. Brown that he fielded from the local media at the league meetings Sunday, opting for a neutral response amid the ongoing trade speculation.

title: Source: Agreed-to Epenesa-Browns deal now off
content: The Cleveland Browns will not be signing AJ Epenesa, with the team uncomfortable finalizing the deal after the veteran defensive end had his physical, a source told ESPN on Sunday.

title: Sources: NFL set to begin hiring replacement refs
content: NFL owners are "alarmed" by the state of negotiations with the NFL Referees Association and have authorized staff members to begin hiring and onboarding replacement officials in the coming weeks, league sources said Sunday.

title: 'Gap' year? Celts win 50th as Brown jabs doubters
content: The Celtics are headed back to the playoffs thanks to another 50-win season. Not too bad for a "gap" year, according to Jaylen Brown.

title: Pitino lands new deal after leading SJU resurgence
content: Rick Pitino has signed a new deal with St. John's that makes him the second-highest-paid coach in the Big East.

title: Woodland wins Houston by 5 amid PTSD battle
content: Gary Woodland won the Houston Open by five shots Sunday, his first PGA Tour victory since undergoing brain surgery in 2023.

title: Mets fans boo Bichette as early strikeouts mount
content: Bo Bichette heard his first boos from Mets fans as he has struck out eight times in his first three games. "I thought my at-bats were terrible, too," he said.

title: 📷 Men's NCAA Sweet 16, Elite Eight superlatives
content: Here are nonstatistical and personality-driven superlatives from the Sweet 16 and Elite Eight rounds of the men's NCAA tournament.

title: How UConn and UCLA clinched a spot in the women's Final Four
content: UCLA rallied to beat Duke and UConn held off Notre Dame as both No. 1 seeds won Sunday in the Elite Eight.

title: NBA playoff watch: With postseason teams set, seeding battles begin
content: Things are getting tight in the East. Plus, updated playoff projections, draft lottery standings after Saturday.

title: WNBA's CBA: 10 biggest wins from new agreement
content: The CBA permanently and positively changes the WNBA's landscape. These are the wins, biggest strides and areas of compromise.

title: Hyo Joo Kim repeats as Ford champ, tops Nelly Korda again
content: Hyo Joo Kim beat Nelly Korda for the second straight week, pulling away around the turn Sunday and closing with a 3-under 69 for a two-shot victory in the Ford Championship.

title: Peyton Manning: Pat Summitt changed my life
content: Legendary NFL quarterback Peyton Manning recounts a career-changing meeting with Pat Summitt that he credits for sparking a lifelong friendship.

title: Predicting top wide receiver prospects' NFL stats: Who ranks No. 1 in the 2026 draft class?
content: Which receivers in the 2026 draft class will have the most success in the pros? We have projections for the top prospects.

title: Man United need to decide soon whether to back Carrick or hire someone else
content: Man United are approaching the point at which they need to decide: Is Michael Carrick our next permanent manager or not? Ian Darke unpacks their dilemma.

title: Time is running out for Foden to make England's World Cup squad
content: In less than 18 months, the Man City playmaker has gone from England mainstay to the fringe of the squad.

title: USMNT handed reality check by Doku, Belgium ahead of World Cup
content: Belgium provided a sobering assessment of where the USMNT currently stands, doling out a heavy 5-2 defeat that will leave Mauricio Pochettino with plenty to ponder.

title: Player ratings: Weah poor as USMNT suffer heavy Belgium defeat
content: It was a difficult game for the U.S. against Belgium, with Tim Weah struggling to make a positive impact.

title: Men's tourney: Arizona, Michigan co-title favorites, Wolverines a slight matchup fave
content: The four remaining team's odds to play for and win the men's college basketball national championship.

title: Chase Elliott gambles, outduels Denny Hamlin at Martinsville
content: Chase Elliott outdueled Denny Hamlin at Martinsville Speedway on Sunday, earning his first NASCAR Cup Series victory of the season.

title: Forget luck: Kimi Antonelli shows he's an F1 title threat in Japan
content: Yes, the timing of a safety car helped Kimi Antonelli to Sunday's win at the Japanese GP, but he was still the fastest car on track when it happened.

title: Moses Itauma isn't the next heavyweight star, his era is already here
content: Moses Itauma's emphatic rise up the heavyweight pecking order continued on Saturday as the boxing world watched in awe at the 21-year-old's knockout over Jermaine Franklin Jr.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Palmer ready to leave Chelsea for Man United?
content: Cole Palmer is becoming increasingly disillusioned at Chelsea and is ready to consider a move to Manchester United. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Draft guide: Rankings, tips, strategy, stats and more
content: All the rankings, projections, cheat sheets, strategy and analysis you need for the fantasy baseball season.

title: Where next for US troops? Iran's 400kg uranium in focus as Trump weighs endgame
content: The US is expanding its military presence in the Middle East with hundreds of additional troops, including special forces, to bolster options amid escalating tensions with Iran. These forces are being positioned to potentially secure the Strait of Hormuz, seize Kharg Island, or target nuclear sites, reflecting a broader posture shift as the conflict evolves.

title: Delhi Police nab Lashkar-e-Taiba commander Shabbir Ahmed Lone near Bangladesh border
content: Delhi Police's Special Cell has apprehended Lashkar-e-Taiba commander Shabbir Ahmed Lone near the India-Bangladesh border. Lone, a key operative, was allegedly operating a LeT module from a hideout near Dhaka. The arrest followed extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering on cross-border terror activities.

title: US market is doomed without my company, says China's fourth richest man to America
content: Chinese battery giant CATL asserts that the US cannot produce electric vehicles without China's involvement, citing its global dominance. Despite US lawmakers' efforts to block Chinese batteries, CATL and BYD remain unfazed, believing protectionist measures will hinder the American auto market.  CATL's technology and low costs have propelled its leadership, with a significant portion of global EVs using its batteries.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ mints over Rs 1,350 crore in 11 days
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has shattered box office records, surpassing its predecessor with a Rs 846.87 crore net collection in India and crossing Rs 1350 crore globally. The film, directed by Aditya Dhar, continues the narrative of Hamza's rise to power after a significant event, weaving in his past identity.

title: Rupee rebounds from record low: Currency rises 128 paise to 93.57 against US dollar
content: The Indian rupee saw a significant rebound, gaining 128 paise to 93.57 against the US dollar following RBI's directive to limit banks' currency exposure. This move prompts banks to reduce dollar holdings, offering temporary support.

title: Google ex-CEO Eric Schmidt says coding is no longer a key skill
content: 0Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt says the best programmers no longer write code—they write specs, set evaluation criteria, and let AI work overnight. Schmidt described a startup programmer whose AI finishes by 4 AM what would have taken him six months and 10 engineers at Google. He argued top programmers will become more valuable, not less, and that AI's biggest impact will be automating costly corporate operations like billing, accounting, and logistics.

title: Record broken! Rohit Sharma dethrones Virat Kohli
content: Rohit Sharma's explosive 78 off 38 balls, coupled with Ryan Rickelton's 81, powered Mumbai Indians to a six-wicket victory over KKR in their IPL 2026 opener. The duo's record 148-run stand secured MI's highest successful home chase. Rohit also set a new IPL record for most runs against a single opponent.

title: Target hit by Iran: Images show destroyed US E-3 Sentry at Saudi Arabia's Prince Sultan base
content: Iran claims to have destroyed a US E-3 Sentry aircraft during a missile and drone strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia. Images show a burned-out E-3 Sentry, a critical airborne surveillance and battle management platform. This incident occurs amidst escalating tensions and retaliatory attacks between Iran, the US, and Israel.

title: Rameez questions PSL ball-tampering on air; Afridi says 'we'll check camera'
content: Lahore Qalandars captain Shaheen Afridi addressed a controversial final over where teammate Fakhar Zaman was accused of ball tampering. The incident led to five penalty runs for the opposition, Karachi Kings, significantly impacting the match's outcome. Afridi stated the team would review the footage to understand the situation fully.

title: Centre rolls out PDS kerosene in 60-day relief measure for consumers amid LPG strain
content: Facing global energy disruptions, the Centre has launched a 60-day emergency plan, distributing extra kerosene to states for cooking and lighting. This move aims to support families experiencing LPG shortages, with authorities also intensifying raids against hoarding and black marketing to ensure supply chain integrity.  Meanwhile, some consumers are switching to piped natural gas.

title: Visakhapatnam horror: Navy technician kills woman, dismembers body, stores parts in fridge
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Vizag as a Naval technician allegedly murdered his girlfriend, dismembered her body, and disposed of some parts. The accused surrendered to police after confessing to the brutal crime. Investigators recovered some body parts from his refrigerator, but the head remains missing, prompting a dedicated search.

title: You may be breathing wrong all day: Doctors explain how it affects your brain& heart
content: Did you know that many of us are caught in a cycle of shallow breathing? This pattern doesn’t just leave us feeling anxious—it can also affect our cognitive abilities and heart health. Dr. Sushil Gupta highlights that shallow breaths can activate our fight-or-flight response, making it hard to concentrate and even causing irregular heart rhythms.

title: How a village potter invented a refrigerator that works without electricity
content: A Gujarati potter, Mansukhbhai Prajapati, revolutionized rural India with his electricity-free clay refrigerator. Born from the aftermath of the 2001 earthquake, this innovative device uses evaporation to keep food and water cool for days. Priced affordably, it offers a vital solution for households facing power cuts and limited resources, proving that practical innovation can emerge from traditional skills.

title: Saudi, Kuwait, Bahrain 'fighting back': Trump claims Gulf nations '100% on US side'
content: President Trump declared Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, Kuwait, and Bahrain are fully aligned with the US against Iran, stating they are "100% on our side." He noted these Gulf nations were surprised by Iran's missile and drone attacks but are now fighting back effectively. The ongoing conflict, now in its fourth week, has seen escalating exchanges and regional instability.

title: IndiGo crew averts disaster as UP man tries to open door mid-air, claims he is ‘possessed’
content: A passenger claiming to be 'possessed' attempted to open an emergency exit door on a Bengaluru-Varanasi IndiGo flight. Crew members swiftly intervened, preventing the man from opening the door on two separate occasions. The pilot temporarily ascended as a precaution before the flight landed safely, and the passenger was detained.

title: Indian worker killed in Iranian attack on Kuwait power, desalination plants
content: “A service building at a power and water desalination plant was attacked as part of the Iranian aggression against the State of Kuwait, resulting in the death of an Indian worker and significant material damage to the building,” ministry spokesperson Fatima Abbas Jawhar Hayat said. Amid rising tensions, Pakistan said it was willing to facilitate and host “meaningful talks” between Washington and Tehran in an effort to end the month-long US-Israel war with Iran.

title: Stock market crash today: Nifty50 opens below 22,500; BSE Sensex down 1,100 points
content: Stock market crash today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex plunged in opening trade on Monday. Nifty50 opened the trading day below 22,500 and BSE Sensex dropped over 1,100 points.

title: ‘Salty, bitter water, cloned phone': Chilling details emerge in Nashik astrologer rape case probe
content: Astrologer Ashok Kharat's police custody has been extended until April 1. Investigators need to analyze his phone data and probe his properties. Kharat faces multiple rape charges. The SIT is also investigating a substance given to a victim. Political figures have exchanged sharp remarks regarding the case and potential threats to Kharat.

title: Ball-tampering in PSL! Fakhar Zaman caught on camera, Afridi reacts - Watch
content: A ball-tampering controversy has erupted in the Pakistan Super League, centering on Lahore Qalandars' Fakhar Zaman. During a crucial final over, Fakhar was seen altering the ball, leading umpires to award five penalty runs to Karachi Kings.  A disciplinary hearing is underway, with a potential one-match ban if found guilty.

title: Videos show Bengaluru Central Prison inmates using mobiles; 3 cops suspended
content: Disturbing footage from Bengaluru Central Prison has surfaced, revealing undertrials allegedly using mobile devices and pointing fingers at DGP Alok Kumar for the supply. An internal inquiry has indicated that the inmates may have intended to derail ongoing jail reforms. Thankfully, the investigation has cleared DGP Kumar of any wrongdoing, with no substantial evidence found.

title: Up, up and away: Is the world running out of Helium gas?
content: Global helium supplies face a critical shortage due to Middle East tensions disrupting production and shipping. This invisible gas, vital for MRIs, semiconductors, and advanced tech, is irreplaceable and lost forever once released. India, heavily reliant on imports, sees soaring prices and potential disruptions in healthcare and its burgeoning chip industry.

title: Bengaluru man kills mother, ailing sister, injures minor over 'mounting debt'
content: A 26-year-old man allegedly attacked his family, resulting in two deaths, due to mounting business debts and his sister's critical illness. He then attempted suicide. The incident, which occurred in Mallenahalli, saw his mother and sister die on the spot, while he and his nephew remain in critical condition.

title: Boy who studied under a lamp, became an IAS officer: What one can learn from his journey
content: Anshuman Raj, from a small village in Bihar, overcame early challenges of limited electricity and financial strain to achieve All India Rank 107 in the UPSC Civil Services Examination 2019. His journey, marked by consistent effort and discipline through public schooling and multiple attempts, highlights that determination can triumph over difficult circumstances.

title: How ‘ghost taxi drivers’ in China tricked customers into paying without rides
content: In an odd twist in the realm of ride-hailing, a group of Chinese drivers resorted to chilling profile pictures to frighten passengers into canceling their rides, allowing them to pocket cancellation fees. Furthermore, some drivers would start rides only to leave without passengers or simply go AWOL, racking up additional penalties.

title: ‘Epstein-like end feared’: Congress flags ‘pen drive bombshell’ in Nashik astrologer case
content: Maharashtra minister Chandrashekhar Bawankule and Congress leader Vijay Wadettiwar clashed over the rape-accused astrologer Ashok Kharat case. Wadettiwar expressed fears for Kharat's safety in custody and demanded action against MLAs who visited him, while Bawankule urged evidence submission to agencies instead of media claims.

title: Baker Mayfield’s Bucs linked to $118M Commanders linebacker deal
content: Following Lavonte David’s retirement annoucement, The Athletic suggests the Tampa Bay Buccaneers target veteran linebacker Bobby Wagner to fill the legendary gap. Wagner is a Super Bowl champion with extensive accolades to his name. He has a high football IQ. He possesses elite run-stopping and pass-rushing abilities. This could potentially fit into Tampa Bay’s needs.

title: Antonio Brown sparks outrage with racist jab at Tiger Woods after DUI
content: Tiger Woods was arrested after a Florida car crash despite a 0.00 breathalyzer result, raising questions about impairment and legal consequences. The situation escalated when Antonio Brown posted, “Tiger Woods drives a car exactly how you would think a black Asian drives”, sparking backlash and shifting focus from concern to controversy online.

title: Hailee Steinfeld shares emotional note before first child with Josh Allen
content: Josh Allen and Hailee Steinfeld are nearing the arrival of their first child, adding a personal dimension to the quarterback’s upcoming season. Steinfeld’s recent newsletter offers a candid glimpse into her life, from honoring her mother to navigating late pregnancy moments. Alongside this, her film Sinners enjoyed award success, with Michael B. Jordan acknowledging her during his Oscar speech. Together, these moments reflect a transition period where career highlights and personal milestones are unfolding side by side.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for this week - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Ipca Laboratories, and AU Small Finance Bank are the stocks that Motilal Oswal Wealth Management Research Desk recommends buying for the week starting March 30, 2026. Target prices and potential upsides are listed below:

title: Aaron Rodgers’ new contract talk sparks debate over TJ Watt’s $123M deal
content: The Pittsburgh Steelers face a significant financial challenge in the 2026 offseason. With T.J. Watt's hefty contract and Aaron Rodgers seeking a substantial raise, the Steelers' cap space is severely strained. Watt's production has declined, raising questions about his value, while Rodgers' strong performance has inflated his market, forcing a tough decision on which star to prioritise. Now, they must choose. Stick with a high-priced defence led by Watt or adjust the books to bring Rodgers back. Either way, the margin for error is gone.

title: Govt works for Indians' safety in Gulf, Congress risking lives: PM Modi
content: ​Launching the BJP-led NDA’s poll campaign at a rally in Kerala’s Palakkad, Modi said many people from the state are working in regions hit by the conflict and “for us their safety remains a top priority”. “Govt is working towards ensuring the crisis impact on India is minimal,” he said.

title: How Praggnanandhaa outfoxed Anish Giri as Indians stay unbeaten in Round 1
content: India's R Praggnanandhaa delivered a tactical masterclass on the opening day of the 2026 FIDE Candidates, defeating Dutch No. 1 Anish Giri. While only three games in the Open section saw decisive results, Praggnanandhaa's clinical precision highlighted a formidable start to his campaign. The Women's section saw all four games end in draws.

title: Oil prices today: Crude jumps as Houthis enter Iran war; US boosts troop presence in Middle East
content: Oil prices surged as the Middle East conflict reached its one-month mark, with Brent crude exceeding $110 and WTI surpassing $100.  The involvement of Iran-aligned Houthis and increased US military presence in the region are fueling market uncertainty and disrupting crucial oil supply routes.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei lauds Iraq's support in new message, remains out of public eye
content: Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed in an airstrike at the start of the war on February 28, has yet to make a public appearance since assuming the role. So far, he has communicated only through a limited number of written statements. His absence from public view has fuelled speculation about his health and whereabouts. However, state television and some Iranian officials have suggested that he is recovering from injuries sustained in an airstrike.

title: IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma scripts history, becomes the first player to…
content: Rohit Sharma achieved a major IPL milestone. He became the first capped player to participate in all 19 seasons. Sharma marked the occasion with a strong batting performance. He scored 78 runs against Kolkata Knight Riders. Mumbai Indians successfully chased a large target. This victory secured a six-wicket win for Mumbai.

title: NFL trade rumors: Miami Dolphins, Los Angeles chargers eye $24M
content: NFL trade rumors are gaining momentum again as Jauan Jennings is attracting significant trade interest from the Miami Dolphins, Los Angeles Chargers, and Titans, with the 49ers having moved on. Jennings offers a valuable skill set, excelling in red zone situations and contested catches, making him a target for teams seeking dependable receiving options. As of now, Jauan Jennings ' situation is very clear. San Francisco has moved on, and three teams have shown interest. The next step now depends on who decides to act first.

title: In Census, 'stable' live-in couple to be counted as married
content: Census 2027 will recognize live-in couples in stable unions as married.  The self-enumeration portal clarifies that such relationships should be treated as married. This marks a public acknowledgment, though officials note similar declarations were accepted in past censuses.  The question is part of the houselisting operations.

title: Trump Plans Ground Op To Extract 400 kg Of Iran's Uranium, But It Has Costs
content: Before the US and Israel launched airstrikes on Iran in June last year, the Islamic Republic was believed to have more than 400 kilograms of 60 per cent highly enriched uranium and nearly 200 kilograms of 20 per cent fissile material.

title: Iran To Gulf: Expanding War Leaves Thousands Dead Across Middle East
content: The US-based Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA) said on March 28 that at least 3,461 people had been killed, including 1,551 civilians

title: Why Is NASA Spending $100 Billion To Return To Moon? Depends On Who You Ask
content: NASA is heading back with its Artemis programme. As early as April 1, the agency will send a crew of four around the moon as a precursor for a landing this decade.

content: New Telangana law requires employees and public representatives to support elderly parents financially.

content: China has acted as a lifeline for North Korea's moribund economy as its largest trading partner and an important source of diplomatic and political support.

content: Pakistan accuses Afghanistan of providing a safe haven for militants who carry out attacks inside Pakistan, especially for the Pakistani Taliban.

title: "Dubai Could Be Blown Up If UAE Enters War," Warns Top US Economist
content: Top US economist Economist Jeffrey Sachs warns UAE against war involvement risking Dubai and Abu Dhabi.

title: US Israel Iran War LIVE: Indian Killed After Iranian Strike Damages Kuwait Power Station
content: US Israel Iran War News LIVE Updates: The United States and Israel continue to strike Iran, whose retaliatory attacks have targeted Israel and neighboring Gulf Arab states. More than 3,000 people have been killed.

content: An analysis of the results of Assembly elections in the State since 1967, shows there have been occasions where candidates lost by fewer than 25 votes

title: Auto drivers protest by climbing cell tower claiming CNG shortage in Hyderabad
content: The protests come amid reports of panic refuelling across the city, which has led to long queues of up to 1 to 2 km at several fuel stations, particularly in residential areas

title: Revisiting Dasgaon, where a 23-year-old drank water from a well and broke a social order
content: Months before the Mahad Satyagraha of 1927, a similar symbolic act by the people of Dasgaon in Maharashtra took place. A group of about 300, led by Ramchandra Babaji More, drew water from a well and a lake and drank it. The satyagrahis were Dalits, implementing a resolution to open public water bodies to them. Now, 100 years later, the people of Dasgaon have forgotten the event

title: Centre allocates PDS kerosene to States, UTs in 60-day emergency measure to ease LPG pressure
content: The move is intended to ensure that households facing LPG constraints continue to have access to essential fuel for basic domestic requirements

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Poll promises may hit the coffers hard
content: While campaigning, LDF is highlighting welfare measures such as increased pensions and monthly wages implemented by the government. UDF has promised five basic ‘welfare guarantees,’ including raising welfare pensions to ₹3,000 if it comes to power. Experts question the rationale of such promises, given the huge cost involved in implementing and sustaining them

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Home voting commences on Monday
content: In the categories of voters above 85 years and persons with disabilities, a little over 2.07 lakh voters have opted for the facility

content: “The decision to field candidates in elections should be left to the leadership of political parties,” he said

content: From Bihar to Rajasthan, thousands of rural workers say work has been halted; some district officials say they have been directed not to start new works as MGNREGA has been discontinued, though there are no such formal orders

content: Two other LPG carriers belonging to the state-owned SCI had crossed the strait and have discharged their cargo in India.

content: If your name is on the electoral roll and your mobile number is registered with the Election Commission of India and linked to your EPIC, you can download your e-EPIC online.

content: The govt. introduced schemes that did not figure in its manifesto, even while delivering key assurances such as Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam and free bus travel; but it has not formulated the Higher Education policy, set up Kalaignar Canteens or created special courts for corruption cases

content: Mr. Ram said that the BJP government under Prime Minister Narendra Modi adopted a “shameful policy” after the “treacherous” attack on Iran by the United States and Israel

content: The Trinamool Congress chairperson urges the electorate to have faith in her; “forget who is your candidate, I am the candidate everywhere”

content: Prime Minister Modi also talked about India’s various cricketing achievements in the recent past, and a number of initiatives being taken up across the country towards nation-building

title: In manifesto, DMK promises homemakers ₹8,000 coupon for buying home appliances of choice
content: Illatharasi scheme will allow homemakers to buy or replace household appliances. It will be the “heroine” of the 2026 election, says DMK president and Chief Minister M.K. Stalin. The manifesto also proposes to extend the Chief Minister’s Breakfast Scheme up to Class 8

title: WTO’s fisheries pact: India for 25 year transition period, stronger disciplines on distant-water industrial fishing
content: The Commerce Ministry said that fisheries subsidies were one of the key agenda items discussed by trade Ministers at the World Trade Organisation’s 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) held from March 26–29 in Yaounde, Cameroon

title: TVK chief Vijay to make electoral debut from Perambur, Tiruchi East constituencies in Tamil Nadu Assembly election
content: TVK general secretary ‘Bussy’ N. Anand will contest from T. Nagar in Chennai, while K.A. Sengottaiyan will test the waters from his home turf, Gobichettipalayam. Aadhav Arjuna will contest from Villivakkam, K.G. Arunraaj from Tiruchengode and C.T.R. Nirmal Kumar from Thirupparankundram.

title: Tea body seeks clarity on Assam land transfer policy for plantation workers
content: Two months ahead of the elections, the BJP-led government amended a law to grant legal land rights to 3.5 lakh families of plantation workers living in the labour lines of more than 800 tea estates

title: West Bengal Assembly polls: Congress names 284 candidates, fields heavyweights
content: Former party leader in Lok Sabha Adhir Ranjan Chowdhury and former Rajya Sabha MP Mausam Benazir Noor are among candidates; Party ends electoral understanding with Left Front

title: Bengaluru metro ridership doubles as IPL ticket–metro integration draws over 22,000 commuters
content: According to a release by BMRCL, the move saw a sharp rise in ridership compared to the usual 8,000–10,000 commuters on match days, while also easing congestion and parking pressure in the central business district.

title: Vijay follows in the footseteps of Tamil Nadu politicians with a penchant for dual contests
content: TVK leader to contest two urban seats in debut election; precedent set by Jayalalithaa, who once contested four seats; PT, DMK, Congress leaders have also attempted dual contests, with less than stellar results

title: Per capita spending varies by 2.5 times across Bengaluru corporations: Report
content: The per capita outlay refers to the amount a corporation is spending on an individual in its jurisdiction

title: Bengaluru Metro services disrupted on Purple Line for maintenance
content: The temporary halt disrupted early morning commuters travelling along the busy Purple Line corridor, as the maintenance announcement came only on Sunday morning when the work began, leaving passengers with no time to alter their plans or arrange alternative modes of transport.

title: M.G. Ramachandran: Tamil Nadu’s charismatic leader who won his first and last elections from hospital bed
content: In 1977, MGR adopted a novel method of campaigning by sending out his life-size cut-outs in jeeps to far-flung villages in his rural Aruppukottai constituency in Ramanathapuram district.

title: Daily flights between Mangaluru-Mumbai Metropolitan Region go up to six, five days in a week
content: IndiGo operates all flights to the Navi Mumbai airport, while it operates two daily flights to the Mumbai airport (BOM), and the Air India Express operates the third daily flight to the BOM

title: ‘A double-engine govt. will de-risk people from  political conflicts that hurt their development’: Rajeev Chandrasekhar
content: If a State government has ‘24x7 animosity towards the Centre, the people will suffer’, says Kerala BJP president Rajeev Chandrasekhar; calling the party’s performance in the 2025 local body elections in the State a ‘big jump forward’, he slams ‘fake narratives’ against the BJP among minorities

title: Former AIADMK functionaries join TVK, fielded in Tirupattur and Cheyyar Assembly seats
content: The AIADMK leadership expelled Dusi K Mohan soon after his candidature was announced by actor C. Joseph Vijay

title: Report highlights steps taken to mitigate risks posed by offshore Virtual Asset Service Providers
content: Listing the measures taken by India, the report said that the FIU has set up a dedicated Working Group with local VASPs and other stakeholders, such as banks, payment aggregators, and gateways, to formulate red flag indicators and develop strategies to detect and address operations of oVASPs serving Indian users

title: News: Concern for Carlos Estevez as Royals' closer blows save
content: fantasy baseball buzz file aims to provide fantasy managers with the intel they need as news breaks around MLB . the first Sunday slate of the season gets underway at 1:35 p.m. ET with three games . concerns about Carlos Estevez 's velocity drop in the spring came to fruition yesterday as the Kansas City Royals ' closer blew a save by yielding six runs in one-third of an inning .

title: Power Rankings: Each franchise's career playoff points leader
content: teams are battling for positioning and playoff spots . some players could add to their playoff point totals . a panel of hockey commentators, analysts, reporters and editors send in a 1-32 poll based on the games through Wednesday .

title: What's next for Duke, Tennessee after Elite Eight: Recruits, transfer portal, more
content: the 2026 men's NCAA tournament has featured its fair share of heartbreaking losses . storied programs didn't reach the regionals such as Kansas and North Carolina . ESPN's Jeff Borzello and Myron Medcalf outline expected departures and returnees .

title: Why NBA insiders are all-in on the Spurs: 'Victor breaks all the rules'
content: the Oklahoma City Thunder ended their longest road trip of the season with a loss to the Boston Celtics . it was the Thunder's first loss in a month . the San Antonio Spurs are a remarkable 23-2 since Feb. 1 .

title: What's next for Duke and Notre Dame after their Elite Eight exit?
content: all four no. 1 seeds advanced to the elite eight of the 2026 women's tournament . the offseason officially starts for some of them . here's your guide to their expected departures, returnees and top incoming recruits .

title: NHL playoff watch: Updated East, West wild-card-race projections
content: in the east, the Boston Bruins and Columbus Blue Jackets hold the precious wild-card positions . they are trailed most closely by the Ottawa Senators and Detroit Red Wings . the Philadelphia Flyers are also in action Sunday (7 p.m. ET, ESPN+ ).
